name: Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Notion for your team

parameters.message: =✅ Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Notion for yo — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 12 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Pulls every page from the Notion database — fine for small datasets; add a filter if the database grows large.

Next: Fetch Changes Since Watermark

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Fetch Changes Since Watermark

GET https://api.example.com/v1/jobs — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Normalize Records

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Normalize Records

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming job records to a stable schema

Next: Upsert into Google Sheets

### Upsert into Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Job Tracker', matched on `jobId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Advance Watermark

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Advance Watermark

Custom JavaScript: Advance the incremental watermark to the newest record seen

Next: Persist Watermark (Notion)

### Persist Watermark (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Next: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Notion for your team

**Pattern:** Workflow Automation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Streamline a multi-step process with HTTP / REST API and Notion for your team. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual process orchestration is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Load Previous Snapshot → Fetch Changes Since Watermark → Normalize Records → Upsert into Google Sheets → Advance Watermark → Persist Watermark (Notion) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to workflow automation without manual effort
- Anyone automating process orchestration in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Standardizes the process every time
- Has retries and error handling built in

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Notion integration token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The pipeline is incremental, not full-refresh: it stores a watermark (the last-seen timestamp) and only asks the source for records newer than that, which is what keeps a scheduled re-run cheap instead of re-pulling the entire dataset every time. The tradeoff is that a failed watermark write must be safe to retry — which is exactly why the destination write is an upsert, not a plain insert.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Manually roll the watermark back and re-run — confirm the upsert makes the replay safe (no duplicates).
2. Trigger a reconcile mismatch on purpose and confirm the drift alarm fires.
3. Point the source at a different platform and see how little else in the workflow has to change.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your process orchestration process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
